body.title: Scraps: debug extractor — log window, state capture, test runner
body.result: debug_extractor.py in devices/scraps/: log window, state snapshot (pe_chain/NE/test/schema), stack trace, error classifier (9 types); 18 tests green
body.description:
Implement the deterministic layer of the /debug skill as Scraps scripts. Given a structured query (component, timestamp window, ticket ID), extract: (1) log window — 20-30 lines around the event timestamp from the appropriate log file; (2) state snapshot — relevant fields for known component types (pe_chain: basket fields; NE: goal + cycle count + last narrative; test failure: assertion + test name; schema error: SQL + table + error); (3) test runner output — structured pass/fail with assertion detail; (4) stack trace parser — file+line+function extraction from raw tracebacks; (5) error classifier — map error pattern to known type (old_string_not_found, test_failure, ne_stuck, schema_error, etc.).

All steps are deterministic — zero inference. Output is a structured dict that T-debug-skill (Haiku/Sonnet layer) consumes.

**Affected files:** devices/scraps/ (new debug_extractor.py script or similar)
**Design rules:** zero inference; all extraction is string parsing, file reads, subprocess calls; structured dict output format must be stable (T-debug-skill depends on it)
**Scope boundary:** extraction only — no hypothesis generation, no report formatting (those are T-debug-skill); handles pe_chain, NE, test, schema error types to start
**Test plan:** given a known log file + timestamp, returns correct 20-30 line window; state snapshot returns correct fields for each component type; error classifier returns correct type for each known pattern
